title: Goldmania paraguensis
intro: Goldmani's orchid (Goldmania paraguensis), also known as Paraguay orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and pain, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms associated with arthritis and other musculoskeletal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Goldmani's orchid include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Goldmani's orchid is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, respiratory infections, and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Goldmania paraguensis is a species of hummingbird in the family Trochilidae. It is endemic to Paraguay. The species is a member of the genus Goldmania. Goldmania paraguensis is closely related to Goldmania leucotis. Goldmania paraguensis is a threatened species due to habitat loss and fragmentation.

constituents: Goldmania paraguensis contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins.
